infobox.value: (born 23 July 1951) is an Australian [jurist](/wiki/Judge "Judge") who is the 39th and current [governor of New South Wales](/wiki/Governor_of_New_South_Wales "Governor of New South Wales"), serving since 2 May 2019. She was the president of the [New South Wales Court of Appeal](/wiki/New_South_Wales_Court_of_Appeal "New South Wales Court of Appeal"), the [first woman to hold the office](/wiki/List_of_the_first_women_appointed_to_Australian_judicial_positions "List of the first women appointed to Australian judicial positions"), from March 2013 until February 2019.[[1]](#cite_note-newgovernor2019-1)

**Margaret Joan Beazley** (born 23 July 1951) is an Australian [jurist](/wiki/Judge "Judge") who is the 39th and current [governor of New South Wales](/wiki/Governor_of_New_South_Wales "Governor of New South Wales"), serving since 2 May 2019. She was the president of the [New South Wales Court of Appeal](/wiki/New_South_Wales_Court_of_Appeal "New South Wales Court of Appeal"), the [first woman to hold the office](/wiki/List_of_the_first_women_appointed_to_Australian_judicial_positions "List of the first women appointed to Australian judicial positions"), from March 2013 until February 2019.[[1]](#cite_note-newgovernor2019-1)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Margaret_Beazley&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Beazley was born and grew up in [Hurstville](/wiki/Hurstville,_New_South_Wales "Hurstville, New South Wales"), [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney"), New South Wales, one of five children to Gordon and Lorna Beazley; her father worked as a milkman. She was educated at Catholic schools – St Declan's Primary School in [Penshurst](/wiki/Penshurst,_New_South_Wales "Penshurst, New South Wales"), St Joseph's Girls High School in [Kogarah](/wiki/Kogarah,_New_South_Wales "Kogarah, New South Wales"), and [Mount Saint Joseph, Milperra](/wiki/Mount_Saint_Joseph,_Milperra "Mount Saint Joseph, Milperra").[[2]](#cite_note-register-2) Beazley graduated from the [Sydney Law School](/wiki/Sydney_Law_School "Sydney Law School") at the [University of Sydney](/wiki/University_of_Sydney "University of Sydney") in 1974 in law, with honours.[[3]](#cite_note-usydnews-3)[[1]](#cite_note-newgovernor2019-1)

## Legal career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Margaret_Beazley&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Legal career")]

Beazley served her articles of clerkship with Winter & Sharp, and was admitted to the [New South Wales Bar](/wiki/New_South_Wales_Bar_Association "New South Wales Bar Association") in 1975. She was appointed [Queen's Counsel](/wiki/Queen%27s_Counsel#Australia "Queen's Counsel") in 1989.[[2]](#cite_note-register-2)

Beazley was a Judicial Member of the Equal Opportunity Tribunal from 1984 to 1988. She was an [Acting District Court Judge](/wiki/District_Court_of_New_South_Wales "District Court of New South Wales") from 1990 to 1991. From 1991 to 1992, Beazley served as Assistant Commissioner of the [Independent Commission Against Corruption](/wiki/Independent_Commission_Against_Corruption_(New_South_Wales) "Independent Commission Against Corruption (New South Wales)"). She was a judge of the Federal Court of Australia from 1993 to 1996, an additional Judge of the [Supreme Court of the Australian Capital Territory](/wiki/Supreme_Court_of_the_Australian_Capital_Territory "Supreme Court of the Australian Capital Territory") from 1994 to 1997, and a judge of the Industrial Relations Court of Australia from 1994 to 1996.[[4]](#cite_note-WhosWho-4)

From 1996 to 2019, she was a Judge of Appeal of the [New South Wales Court of Appeal](/wiki/New_South_Wales_Court_of_Appeal "New South Wales Court of Appeal"). She was President of the court, the [first woman to hold the office](/wiki/List_of_the_first_women_appointed_to_Australian_judicial_positions "List of the first women appointed to Australian judicial positions"), from 2013[[5]](#cite_note-5) until February 2019.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Beazley has also served on the Australian Executive of [Amnesty International](/wiki/Amnesty_International "Amnesty International") (1980).

## Governor of New South Wales

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Margaret_Beazley&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Governor of New South Wales")]

In January 2019, Beazley accepted the position of 39th [Governor of New South Wales](/wiki/Governor_of_New_South_Wales "Governor of New South Wales"),[[8]](#cite_note-8) succeeding [David Hurley](/wiki/David_Hurley "David Hurley"),[[9]](#cite_note-9) and was sworn in as governor on 2 May 2019.[[1]](#cite_note-newgovernor2019-1)[[10]](#cite_note-10) In 2020, she was promoted to [Companion of the Order of Australia](/wiki/Companion_of_the_Order_of_Australia "Companion of the Order of Australia") for eminent service to the people of New South Wales, particularly through leadership roles in the judiciary, and as a mentor of young women lawyers.[[11]](#cite_note-iahref-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12)

In November 2021, it was reported that a member of the governor's staff had anonymously lodged whistleblower complaints about bullying with the [Department of Premier and Cabinet](/wiki/Department_of_Premier_and_Cabinet_(New_South_Wales) "Department of Premier and Cabinet (New South Wales)"), which provides staff for the governor's office. Following an investigation, the Department—while not referring to a decision—thanked the complainant and promised measures to foster a "respectful" workplace culture in the office.[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)

Beazley's term in office was extended in March 2024 to May 2026[[15]](#cite_note-15) and then her term was extended again in January 2026 to 1 May 2028.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

## Honours and awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Margaret_Beazley&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Honours and awards")]

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | [Companion of the Order of Australia](/wiki/Companion_of_the_Order_of_Australia "Companion of the Order of Australia") (AC) | 2020[[11]](#cite_note-iahref-11) |
| [Officer of the Order of Australia](/wiki/Officer_of_the_Order_of_Australia "Officer of the Order of Australia") (AO) | 2006[[17]](#cite_note-AO-17) |
|  | Dame of Grace of the [Order of St John of Jerusalem](/wiki/Order_of_Saint_John_(chartered_1888) "Order of Saint John (chartered 1888)") | 2019[[18]](#cite_note-18) |

### Honorary appointments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Margaret_Beazley&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Honorary appointments")]

* [![Australia](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/88/Flag_of_Australia_%28converted%29.svg/40px-Flag_of_Australia_%28converted%29.svg.png)](/wiki/Australia "Australia") 2019: Honorary Colonel of the [Royal New South Wales Regiment](/wiki/Royal_New_South_Wales_Regiment "Royal New South Wales Regiment").[[19]](#cite_note-19)
* [![New South Wales](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/00/Flag_of_New_South_Wales.svg/40px-Flag_of_New_South_Wales.svg.png)](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales") 2019: Governor of the [New South Wales Police Force](/wiki/New_South_Wales_Police_Force "New South Wales Police Force").[[20]](#cite_note-20)
* [![United Kingdom](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a5/Flag_of_the_United_Kingdom_%281-2%29.svg/40px-Flag_of_the_United_Kingdom_%281-2%29.svg.png)](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") 2019: [Deputy Prior of the Order of St John](/wiki/Order_of_Saint_John_(chartered_1888) "Order of Saint John (chartered 1888)").[[21]](#cite_note-21)
* [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/38/Naval_Ensign_of_Australia.svg/40px-Naval_Ensign_of_Australia.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Naval_Ensign_of_Australia.svg) 2019: Honorary [Commodore](/wiki/Commodore_(Royal_Navy) "Commodore (Royal Navy)"), [Royal Australian Navy](/wiki/Royal_Australian_Navy "Royal Australian Navy").[[22]](#cite_note-22)
* [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/38/Air_Force_Ensign_of_Australia.svg/40px-Air_Force_Ensign_of_Australia.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Air_Force_Ensign_of_Australia.svg) 2019: Honorary [Air Commodore](/wiki/Air_Commodore "Air Commodore") of [No. 22 Squadron](/wiki/No._22_Squadron_RAAF "No. 22 Squadron RAAF") [Royal Australian Air Force](/wiki/Royal_Australian_Air_Force "Royal Australian Air Force").[[23]](#cite_note-23)

### Honorary degrees

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Margaret_Beazley&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Honorary degrees")]

* [![New South Wales](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/00/Flag_of_New_South_Wales.svg/40px-Flag_of_New_South_Wales.svg.png)](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales") 2008: Honorary Doctorate of Laws (LLD) by the [University of Sydney](/wiki/University_of_Sydney "University of Sydney").[[24]](#cite_note-24)
* [![New South Wales](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/00/Flag_of_New_South_Wales.svg/40px-Flag_of_New_South_Wales.svg.png)](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales") 2019: Honorary Doctorate of Laws (LLD) by the [Australian Catholic University](/wiki/Australian_Catholic_University "Australian Catholic University").[[25]](#cite_note-25)
* [![New South Wales](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/00/Flag_of_New_South_Wales.svg/40px-Flag_of_New_South_Wales.svg.png)](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales") 2022: Honorary Doctorate of Laws (LLD) by [The University of Notre Dame Australia](/wiki/The_University_of_Notre_Dame_Australia "The University of Notre Dame Australia").[[26]](#cite_note-26)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Margaret_Beazley&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Beazley is married to Dennis Wilson. She has three adult children from her first marriage to barrister Alan Sullivan.[[27]](#cite_note-27)[[28]](#cite_note-28) She and Sullivan were reportedly "the first husband and wife from the private legal profession to become QCs".[[29]](#cite_note-29)
